topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias leaned back in his ergonomic chair, the leather sighing beneath his weight. The afternoon sun slanted across his desk, illuminating dust motes dancing in the air. He was reviewing quarterly reports, a comfortable, slow rhythm taking over his focus. Then, a notification popped up on his screen—a salary comparison spreadsheet he hadn't intended to open. His eyes scanned the numbers, a slight furrow appearing between his brows as he cross-referenced the figures for the junior analyst, Sarah. A slow, almost imperceptible smile touched the corners of his mouth as he realized the discrepancy. He clicked away, the tension draining from his shoulders as he accepted the new reality.

[story 2]

Maya stretched languidly, letting her spine pop with a satisfying crack. The hum of the office air conditioning was a soothing backdrop to her contemplation. She was halfway through a lukewarm cup of tea, her gaze drifting toward the cubicle farm where Ben worked. When she saw the pay stub comparison pop up on her monitor, she paused, her hand hovering over the mouse. A quiet chuckle escaped her lips, a soft sound that barely disturbed the quiet hum of the workspace. She leaned back again, taking a slow, deep breath, letting the information settle without immediate reaction. It was just a fact, nothing more.

[story 3]

For David, the evening felt particularly easy. He was scrolling through personal news feeds, a faint, contented smile playing on his lips as he watched amusing anecdotes unfold. He stumbled upon the internal compensation data while browsing a shared drive. His initial reaction was a brief flicker of surprise, quickly smoothed over by a feeling of detached amusement. He reread the figures, his posture settling further into the chair. He picked up his phone, intending to text someone, but instead, he just stared at the screen for a moment longer, a sense of quiet acceptance washing over him. The world, for now, was perfectly fine.
